CALENDAR GUIDE

Does the Week Start on Sunday or Monday?

It depends on where you are. The international ISO 8601 standard defines Monday as the first day of the week. But the United States, Canada, much of Latin America, and Japan traditionally start their week on Sunday.

Calendars
Week Numbers 2025·Week Numbers 2026·Week Numbers 2027

Sunday-first vs Monday-first countries

First day of weekCountries / Regions
MondayMost of Europe, UK, Australia, China, Russia, most of Africa, ISO 8601 standard
SundayUnited States, Canada, Mexico, Brazil, Japan, South Korea, most of Latin America
SaturdaySome Islamic countries (Maldives, Afghanistan)

Why the difference?

The difference traces back to two traditions:

  • Abrahamic tradition: The Sabbath (day of rest) is the last day of the week — Saturday in Judaism, Sunday in Christianity. This makes Sunday the "first" day of the new week in religious calendars, which influenced US and Latin American calendars.
  • ISO 8601 / European tradition: The international standard defines Monday as day 1 and Sunday as day 7, reflecting the secular work week pattern common in Europe.

Impact on calendars and software

This difference affects how calendars display weeks:

  • US calendars and Google Calendar (US locale) show Sunday as the first column
  • European calendars and ISO-compliant apps show Monday first
  • In spreadsheets: =WEEKDAY(A1, 2) uses Monday=1 (ISO mode)

FAQ

What does ISO 8601 say about the first day of the week?

ISO 8601 defines Monday as day 1 and Sunday as day 7. This is the international standard used in business, logistics, and most of Europe.

Why does the US week start on Sunday?

The US convention traces back to the Judeo-Christian tradition where Sunday is the first day of the new week, following the Sabbath (Saturday).

Does Google Calendar start the week on Sunday or Monday?

Google Calendar defaults to Sunday in the US locale. You can change it in Settings → View options → Start week on → Monday.

In the Bible, what is the first day of the week?

In the Bible, Sunday is the first day of the week. Genesis describes God resting on the seventh day (Sabbath = Saturday), making Sunday the start of the new week.

Related: Days of the week: name origins · ISO week number explained · What day of the week was I born?
